title: The Hidden Talent
content:
Chen Fan's display in the training yard quickly spread throughout the Outer Sect. Whispers of the new disciple with mysterious sword skills filled every corner.

"Have you seen Chen Fan practice? His sword moves are unlike anything I've ever seen!"
"He must have learned from some secret master before joining the sect."
"Impossible! He's only been here a week!"

Chen Fan ignored the gossip, focusing instead on his daily training. Every morning, he rose before dawn to practice the Heavenly Sword Technique in secret. Every afternoon, he sparred with other disciples to hone his combat skills.

One day, while practicing alone in the woods, a voice interrupted him.

"Interesting... Very interesting."

Chen Fan turned to see a burly young man standing behind a tree, his arms crossed. He was about fifteen, with a round face and a friendly grin.

"Who are you?" Chen Fan asked, keeping his guard up.

"Wang Tieshan!" the boy said, stepping forward. "They call me Iron Mountain because of my strength. I saw you practicing earlier - your sword skills are amazing!"

Chen Fan relaxed slightly. Wang Tieshan's energy was warm and genuine, nothing like the cold calculating eyes of Zhang Wei in his later years.

"Thanks," Chen Fan said. "I've been practicing for a while."

"Mind if I join you?" Wang Tieshan asked. "I could use a good sparring partner. Most of the other disciples are too weak to keep up with me."

Chen Fan nodded, and the two began to spar. Wang Tieshan lived up to his nickname - his attacks were powerful and straightforward, like a mountain crashing down. But Chen Fan's sword was faster, more precise, and he easily dodged Wang Tieshan's strikes.

"Wow!" Wang Tieshan said, gasping for breath after their match. "You're incredible! How did you get so good?"

"Lots of practice," Chen Fan said with a smile.

Over the next few weeks, Chen Fan and Wang Tieshan became fast friends. They trained together every day, pushing each other to improve. Wang Tieshan's strength complemented Chen Fan's speed, and they learned from each other.

Meanwhile, Chen Fan's relationship with Zhang Wei began to change. At first, Zhang Wei had been excited to see his friend's progress, but as Chen Fan continued to outshine him, a subtle jealousy began to creep in.

"Chen Fan, can you show me that move again?" Zhang Wei asked one afternoon, watching Chen Fan practice.

"Sure," Chen Fan said, demonstrating the technique.

But when Zhang Wei tried to copy it, his movements were stiff and awkward. He cursed under his breath.

"It's okay, Wei'er," Chen Fan said. "You just need more practice."

"Easy for you to say," Zhang Wei muttered, walking away.

Chen Fan watched him go, a strange feeling in his chest. This was the beginning - the moment Zhang Wei's jealousy started to grow. In his past life, this had led to betrayal. But this time...

Chen Fan shook his head. He wouldn't let history repeat itself. He would keep an eye on Zhang Wei, but he wouldn't let suspicion consume him.

That night, as Chen Fan was meditating in his room, there was a knock on the door.

"Enter," he said.

Elder Li stepped in, his expression serious.

"Chen Fan," he said, "I have something to tell you."

"What is it, Elder?"

"The Sect Master has heard about your progress," Elder Li said. "He wants to see you."

Chen Fan's heart skipped a beat. The Sect Master wanted to see him? That was unheard of for a new Outer Sect disciple.

"When?"

"Tomorrow morning," Elder Li said. "Be prepared."

As Elder Li left, Chen Fan couldn't help but feel a sense of foreboding. In his past life, he hadn't met the Sect Master until much later. Why was he being summoned now?

He shook off the feeling. Whatever the reason, he would face it head-on.

This was his chance to change his fate, and he wasn't going to waste it.
